data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Design interface elements for ease of access, emphasizing target size and distance for interaction.
data.technical_spec: Optimized button sizes and spacing for mobile usability.

data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Minimizes decision-making time by reducing the complexity of visible content and navigation.
data.technical_spec: Streamlined navigation and quick access to primary features.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Keeps information chunks manageable to avoid overwhelming users with too much information at once.
data.technical_spec: Layout designs that focus on three to four key features per screen.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Designs user experiences based on familiar interfaces that align with user expectations.
data.technical_spec: Consistency in UI elements like stories, feed layout, and direct messages.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Ensures aesthetically pleasing designs to enhance perceived usability.
data.technical_spec: High-resolution images, balanced color schemes, and modern typography.
